description: Complete map of the MCP subsystem across all packages — config, auth, transport, tools, desktop persistence, and app polling.

packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts.role: Core MCP service — client lifecycle, tool listing, OAuth flow, connection management

variants.note: carries exit code for process failures

methods.name: add
methods.signature: (name: string, mcp: ConfigMCP.Info) => Effect.Effect<{ status: Record<string, Status> | Status }>

methods.name: getPrompt
methods.signature: (clientName: string, name: string, args?: Record<string, string>) => Effect.Effect<Awaited<ReturnType<MCPClient["getPrompt"]>> | undefined>

methods.name: startAuth
methods.signature: (mcpName: string) => Effect.Effect<{ authorizationUrl: string; oauthState: string }, NotFoundError>

module_level_mutable_state.name: pendingOAuthTransports
module_level_mutable_state.purpose: Holds StreamableHTTP/SSE transports that are awaiting OAuth completion before client.connect() is called. Keyed by MCP server name.

module_level_mutable_state.name: serverLocks
module_level_mutable_state.purpose: Per-server mutex to serialize connect/disconnect operations. Each server gets exactly one Semaphore(1). Created lazily via withServerLock().

listener_registration.details: In watch() function (line 531): client.setNotificationHandler(ToolListChangedNotificationSchema, () => ...). This handler re-lists tools via listTools() on tool-list-changed notification, then emits ToolsChanged bus event. No cleanup mechanism for this handler — it persists for client lifetime.
listener_registration.cleanup: closeClient() calls client.close() which should clean up transport-level listeners, but the notification handler on the MCP client is not explicitly removed.

process_env_usage.direct: process.env is NOT read directly in index.ts
process_env_usage.indirect: StdioClientTransport spawns child processes with configurable env (from ConfigMCP.Local.environment). The env is passed to StdioClientTransport constructor as `env: { ...process.env, ...(mcp.environment ?? {}) }`. This means process.env is spread into every child process spawn — all 60+ env vars are leaked to MCP subprocesses.

patterns.usage: listTools wraps client.listTools() in Effect.tryPromise with explicit catch mapping
listTools has a fallback: on output schema validation error, retries with TolerantListToolsResultSchema (no outputSchema validation)
connect() catches UnauthorizedError to trigger OAuth flow, catches NotFoundError to return typed error
In add() when connecting auto-enabled servers: .pipe(Effect.ignore) on individual connect errors so one failing server doesn't block others
NotFoundError extends Schema.TaggedErrorClass for typed error channels
Failed error wraps any unknown error with MCP server name context
connectTransport calls .pipe(Effect.orDie) — if transport start fails, the fiber dies (not recoverable)

child_process_spawning.note: Uses ChildProcessSpawner/CrossSpawnSpawner from effect/unstable/process as a dependency for observing child process PID and exit status, but the actual spawn is done by the MCP SDK's StdioClientTransport, not by Effect's process module directly.

steps: 1. add(name, mcp) — creates transport, connects, registers tools/prompts/resources, sets HealthRegistry status
2. connectTransport(transport, timeout) — calls transport.start() + client.connect(transport), both with Effect.orDie
3. For remote with OAuth: creates McpOAuthProvider, sets transport.sessionId, triggers authorization flow
4. watch(s, name, client, bridge, timeout) — sets ToolListChangedNotification handler, re-lists tools on change
5. closeClient(s, name) — calls client.close(), clears HealthRegistry and InstanceState, removes from state.machines

health_integration.usage: Each server connection updates HealthRegistry with status: "ok" (connected), "degraded" (connecting/reconnecting), "error" (failed/disconnected). PID tracked for stdio transports.

packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts.role: File-backed OAuth token and client information storage

methods.name: set
methods.signature: (mcpName: string, entry: Entry, serverUrl?: string) => Effect.Effect<void>

methods.name: updateTokens
methods.signature: (mcpName: string, tokens: Tokens, serverUrl?: string) => Effect.Effect<void>

methods.name: updateClientInfo
methods.signature: (mcpName: string, clientInfo: ClientInfo, serverUrl?: string) => Effect.Effect<void>

methods.name: isTokenExpired
methods.note: returns null if no tokens, false if no expiresAt, true if expired

patterns.usage: all() catches any error reading/parsing the JSON file and returns empty object — file-not-found is silently tolerated
set() and remove() both call .pipe(Effect.orDie) on fs.writeJson — if auth file write fails, the fiber dies
Schema.decodeUnknownOption is used to decode the JSON data, so malformed entries are silently filtered out rather than causing errors

packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-provider.role: OAuth client provider implementation for MCP SDK
packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-provider.effect_bridge: Uses Effect.runPromise to call into the McpAuth Effect service from the synchronous OAuthClientProvider interface. Every async method wraps auth calls in try/catch with log.error on failure.

error_handling.usage: Every method that calls auth service uses try/catch wrapping Effect.runPromise. Errors are logged but some methods re-throw (codeVerifier throws on missing code verifier, state generates new state on missing).

clientInformation() returns undefined on errors, tokens() returns undefined on errors, invalidateCredentials() returns void on errors — all failure modes are silently swallowed at the OAuthClientProvider boundary
codeVerifier() throws Error if no code verifier is stored — this propagates to the MCP SDK which handles it

packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-provider.url_scoped_credentials: Uses getForUrl(mcpName, serverUrl) to validate tokens/clientInfo match the current server URL. If serverUrl changes, stored credentials are invalidated.

packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.role: Local HTTP server to receive OAuth redirect callbacks

exports.name: waitForCallback
exports.signature: function waitForCallback(oauthState: string, mcpName?: string): Promise<string>

fields.starting: Promise<void> | undefined (dedup guard for concurrent ensureRunning calls)

module_level_mutable_state.note: Wrapped in a mutable container rather than bare module-level variables. The comment says this is intentional: 'prevents accidental reassignment and makes concurrent-access guards easier to reason about.'

listener_registration.server: createServer(handleRequest). srv.on('error', reject) during startup, then srv.listen(port, resolve).
listener_registration.connection_probing: isPortInUse uses createConnection with socket.on('connect')/socket.on('error') — sockets are destroyed after check but event listeners are not explicitly removed.
listener_registration.timeout_callbacks: Each PendingAuth has a 5-minute setTimeout. timeout is cleared on successful callback, cancellation, or server stop.

packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.csrf_protection: Enforces state parameter presence. Rejects callbacks missing state param. Validates state exists in pendingAuths map before resolving.

error_handling.usage: ensureRunning wraps listen in try/catch/finally — resets state.server to undefined on failure, resets state.starting to undefined in finally
5-minute CALLBACK_TIMEOUT_MS. Pending promises are rejected with 'OAuth callback timeout - authorization took too long'
stop() iterates all pending auths, clears timeouts, and rejects with 'OAuth callback server stopped'

packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.note: This is entirely imperative/stateful code with no Effect integration. It manages a singleton HTTP server with mutable pending-auth state.

packages/opencode/src/config/mcp.ts.role: Schema definitions for MCP configuration — pure data, no behavior

exports.name: Local
exports.description: Local MCP server spawned as a child process via command+args

exports.name: Remote
exports.description: Remote MCP server connected via HTTP/SSE transport

packages/opencode/src/config/mcp.ts.process_env_usage: None — does not parse process.env. Environment variables are configured via the Local.environment field, which is applied downstream in StdioClientTransport.
packages/opencode/src/config/mcp.ts.error_handling: None — this is a pure schema file. All validation is deferred to Effect Schema decode at config load time.

packages/opencode/src/session/tools.ts.role: Session tool resolution — builds the AI tool registry including MCP tools

exports.name: resolve
exports.signature: (input: { agent, model, session, processor, bypassAgentCheck, messages, promptOps }) => Effect.Effect<Record<string, AITool>>

flow: 1. Calls yield* mcp.tools() to get all connected MCP server tools
3. For each MCP tool: calls asSchema(item.inputSchema).jsonSchema to get JSON schema
4. Applies ProviderTransform.schema(input.model, schema) for model-specific schema adaptation
5. Wraps execute in Effect.gen with: permission check (ctx.ask), Effect.promise(execute(args, opts)), plugin triggers (before/after)
6. Wraps the entire execute in run.promise() via EffectBridge for ai-sdk integration
7. Converts MCP result content: text→textParts, image→file attachment with data: URI, resource→textParts + file attachment
8. Applies truncate.output() on the combined text output
mcp_tool_registration.permission_check: ctx.ask({ permission: key, metadata: {}, patterns: ['*'], always: ['*'] }) — MCP tools always ask permission with wildcard patterns

concurrency.toolSemaphore: Uses readSemaphore for cacheable tools, writeSemaphore for non-cacheable tools

error_handling.usage: Permission.ask() pipes with Effect.orDie — if permission system fails, the fiber dies
All tool executions (builtin + MCP) are wrapped in TypedResult so failures carry typed error info
After MCP tool execution, checks (result as { isError?: boolean })?.isError to determine mcpStatus as 'failed' or 'succeeded'

packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-config.ts.role: Electron IPC handlers for persisting MCP server configuration in electron-store

exports.name: registerConfigIpcHandlers
exports.note: Registers ipcMain.handle() for CONFIG channels — side-effect only, called once at app init

GET_DESKTOP_MCP_SERVERS.error_handling: try/catch → console.error → rethrow → withIpcResult wraps in IpcErr envelope

SET_DESKTOP_MCP_SERVERS.flow: validateAndFilterMcpServers(servers) → serializedWrite('desktop-mcp-servers', () => store.set('servers', v.servers)) → returns { servers, dropped }

packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-config.ts.note: NO DELETE handler for MCP servers. NO push-based notification to renderer. The renderer must poll GET_DESKTOP_MCP_SERVERS. This file handles configuration persistence only — it does NOT track runtime connection status.

packages/app/src/context/server-sync.tsx.role: App-level server synchronization context — polls MCP status via @tanstack/solid-query

exports.name: loadMcpQuery
exports.note: tanstack query options: queryKey=[directory, 'mcp'], queryFn: () => sdk.mcp.status().then(r => r.data ?? {})

exports.name: createServerSyncContext
exports.note: Creates the full sync context with store, queries, bootstrapping, etc.

mcp_polling.note: MCP status is polled through the generated SDK which calls the sidecar REST API. The query is refetched when the directory sync triggers a refresh.

child_store_mcp.role: Per-directory SolidJS store with { mcp: Record<string, Status> } state

error_handling.usage: Query failures surface through @tanstack/solid-query error state

packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts.role: Auto-generated SDK client class for MCP REST endpoints

methods.name: list
methods.note: same endpoint as status, different response interpretation

desktop.concern: Persists MCP server config to electron-store. No runtime status tracking. No push notifications.

app.concern: Polls MCP status through SDK REST client. Caches per-directory in SolidJS stores.

opencode_mcp.concern: Core MCP service: client lifecycle, tool listing, OAuth flow, auth persistence, config schema.

opencode_session.concern: Registers MCP tools into the AI tool registry with permission checks, concurrency control, and output truncation.

sdk.concern: Auto-generated REST client for MCP endpoints consumed by the app.

edges.via: shared McpServerEntry type in preload/types.ts (parallel to ConfigMCP)

process_env_leak.description: When spawning local MCP servers, process.env is spread into the child process environment via StdioClientTransport({ env: { ...process.env, ...(mcp.environment ?? {}) } }). All 60+ env vars from the parent process (including secrets, tokens, API keys) are exposed to every MCP server subprocess.

servers.safety: Accessed within the Effect runtime via withServerLock semaphore — but the Map itself is a bare JS Map outside Effect Ref
Lazily created. Semaphore instances are Effect-managed but the outer Map is bare JS
Pure imperative code. No synchronization primitives. Concurrent ensureRunning() calls are deduplicated via state.starting guard, but pendingAuths mutations are not locked

duplicate_config_schema.description: Two parallel schemas exist for MCP config: ConfigMCP.Info (Effect Schema, in packages/opencode/src/config/mcp.ts) and McpServerEntry (TypeScript type, in packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts). These must be kept in sync manually. The desktop ipc-validation.ts also has its own ad-hoc validation rather than using the Effect Schema decode.

no_push_notification.description: Desktop has no IPC push channel for MCP status changes. The app must poll. The index.ts emits ToolsChanged and BrowserOpenFailed bus events internally, but these are not bridged to the desktop IPC layer.
